stated_strategic_priorities: Scale customized separate account (SMA) platform as the core client-acquisition and asset-retention engine, offering bespoke portfolio construction across private markets
Expand evergreen and semi-liquid fund structures to democratize private-markets access for wealth-channel and retail investors
Leverage proprietary data, analytics, and technology platform to differentiate advisory relationships and drive operational alpha across $929B+ in assets under management and supervision

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: Hamilton Lane operates across the ~$14T global private markets allocation opportunity, with a focus on the $2T+ institutional outsourced private-markets solutions segment and the nascent but rapidly growing wealth-channel private-markets market projected to reach $1.5T+ by 2030. The firm's total capital under management and supervision of ~$929B provides a data advantage that is difficult for smaller competitors to replicate.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Dual-class share structure with Class B shares held by Hamilton Lane Advisors, LLC (the pre-IPO partnership), concentrating voting control with senior managing directors. IPO in March 2017 on NASDAQ. The partnership structure preserves founder and senior-team alignment but limits outside governance influence. Hartley Rogers serves as Executive Chairman and Mario Giannini served as long-time CEO before Erik Hirsch became CEO in 2024.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: SEC-registered investment adviser. Subject to Investment Advisers Act of 1940. Evergreen and interval fund products regulated under the Investment Company Act of 1940. Global operations require compliance with FCA (UK), BaFin (Germany), MAS (Singapore), and other local regulatory frameworks across 20+ offices worldwide.
